compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary

While certifying closes during the config-prune/identity epic, ticket READS disagreed with the authoritative on-disk state, causing a long misdiagnosis (I believed closes weren't signing when they were). The user's hunch — and mine — is a CACHING layer serving stale ticket state relative to freshly-written events, likely affecting other ticket operations too. This is a scoped INVESTIGATION for a separate session, not a known-root-cause fix.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Close a work ticket so the completion-verifier signs an attestation (in_progress -> closed, gate on).
2. Immediately read it two ways:
   - `rebar show <id> --output llm` and inspect the `attestations` map.
   - `rebar verify-signature <id> --kind completion-verifier`.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** both agree — if verify-signature certifies, `show`'s attestations map should include the `completion-verifier` entry.
- **Actual (observed this session):** `show --output llm` reported `attestations: {}` (empty) for the epic 8d27, 297d, 1787, and ad48, while `rebar verify-signature <id> --kind completion-verifier` reported **'certified'** for all four, and SIGNATURE.json events were present on disk in each ticket dir. So the signed attestation existed + certified, but the show read-path did not reflect it. (Note: earlier in the session, `show --output llm` DID surface attestations for another ticket (c366) — so the inconsistency is state/timing-dependent, not a flat format gap.)

## Corroborating second data point (same 'stale cache' family)

The reconciler inbound-signing test (test_inbound_signing_9e76) had to call `_seam._ATTRIBUTION_CACHE.clear()` before driving `_write_event_file`: an identity created mid-process was NOT resolved because attribution is cached per-repo_root for the process lifetime (a pre-identity miss was cached). A real reconciler process starts with the identity already present so it's fine in prod, but it confirms a per-process cache that can serve stale identity/attribution state.

## Hypotheses to investigate (separate session)

- The `show` / reducer read-path may serve a cached/compacted SNAPSHOT that lags freshly-appended events (SIGNATURE events written at close), so the attestations map renders stale/empty until the cache is invalidated or the snapshot recompacted.
- Per-process caches (`_ATTRIBUTION_CACHE`, any reducer/state cache) may not invalidate on same-process writes (compaction-on-close, reopen->close cycles, sign_manifest).
- Interaction with compact-on-close: closing compacts the ticket; the attestation is written around/after compaction — a race or cache-ordering issue could drop it from the read view.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified for why `rebar show --output llm` `attestations` disagreed with `verify-signature` / on-disk SIGNATURE events (reproduce deterministically).
- [ ] Determine the scope: which ticket read operations (show, list, ready, next-batch, deps, --output llm vs default) can serve stale state relative to just-written events, and under what triggers (compact-on-close, reopen->close, in-process writes).
- [ ] Either fix the read-path to be consistent with the store (invalidate/refresh the cache after writes) or document the invariant + a supported way to force a fresh read.
- [ ] Regression test that a freshly-signed completion attestation is visible in the same read surfaces that verify-signature certifies.
compiled_state.title: Ticket read-path shows stale state vs on-disk/verify — completion attestations appear ABSENT in `show` though verify-signature certifies (caching?)
